Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd (In Liquidation)

Facts.Text: By 2007, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd was involved in three lines of business: outer port limit offshore supply vessel services around Singapore, ship management, and ship chartering.

From the financial year ending 31 December 2002 onwards, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd showed a negative equity position on its balance sheet, which increased in magnitude over subsequent years up to at least 31 March 2011.
In August 2007, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd obtained a loan of US$1,000,000 from Parakou Shipping SA to finance construction of new outer port limit vessels.
In September 2007, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd entered into a shipbuilding contract of about S$6,000,000 for delivery of outer port limit vessels from August 2008 to January 2009.
In September 2007, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd obtained a loan of S$6,120,000 from Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd.
In February 2008, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd obtained a loan of S$1,000,000 from Parakou Shipping SA to finance construction of new outer port limit vessels.

A document titled 'Minutes of a meeting of the Board of Directors' dated 17 March 2008 and signed by Liu Cheng Chan as chairman recorded that for the purpose of restructuring, Liu Cheng Chan and Chik Sau Kam would transfer all their shares to Liu Por and Yang Jianguo within 2008, and that the company would terminate the operation of OPL boats and ship management.

On 10 June 2008, it was reported to Yang that Parakou had entered into a new fixture for 5 years with Galsworthy Limited as disponent owners, and that the Canton Trader would be relet to sub-charterers Ocean Glory on a strict back-to-back basis.
On 17 June 2008, Clarkson Asia Limited sent Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd a 'Clean Recap' for the Canton Trader to conclude a clean fixture between Parakou and Galsworthy Limited.
On 17 June 2008, Ocean Glory Shipping Ltd sent Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd a similar 'Clean Recap' for the Canton Trader to 'conclude the clean fixture' for the sub-charterparty.
On 17 July 2008, Clarkson sent Parakou a copy of the charterparty for the Canton Trader signed by Galsworthy.
By the end of October 2008, the Baltic Dry Index had fallen further to about 770 points, a historical low, indicating severe deterioration in the dry bulk freight market.
On 30 October 2008, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd received an email warning it to pay close attention to the financial condition of Ocean Glory Shipping Ltd.
On 31 October 2008, Clarkson Asia Limited sent Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd the original charterparty documents for the Canton Trader.
On 14 November 2008, CC Liu and Chik, as directors of Parakou, passed a directors' resolution authorising Liu Por and Yang to execute documents for the sale of ten vessels and two hulls.
On 17 November 2008, Liu Por and Yang signed the documents for the sale of the OPL Vessels to PIH.
On 17 November 2008, CC Liu and Chik, in their capacities as directors of PIH, signed a directors' resolution resolving to purchase the OPL Vessels from Parakou.
On 18 November 2008, PSMPL was incorporated in Singapore. Its businesses comprise shipping lines and freight.
Subsequently, 12 ship management agreements between Parakou and 12 companies (the 'Pretty' entities) were terminated with effect from 30 November 2008.
The Pretty Entities entered into SMAs with PSMPL for management with effect from 1 December 2008 on terms substantially similar to those with Parakou.
On 22 December 2008, CC Liu and Chik transferred their shares in Parakou to Liu Por and Yang, appointed them as directors, and gave notice of their resignation.
Between 12 and 24 November 2008, Parakou repaid PIH a total amount of S$9,812,542.80 that was owing to PIH.
On 9 and 15 December 2008, Parakou set off a total amount of S$1,732,239.17 owing from PIH against amounts owed by Parakou to PIH.
On 5 December 2008, Parakou repaid a sum of S$3,046,200 that was owing to PSSA.
On 23 December 2008, Liu Por and Yang signed a resolution noting that 39 of Parakou's employees would be relieved of their duties with effect from 31 December 2008.
In January 2009, the Affected Employees were employed by PSMPL.
Between January 2009 and December 2010, Parakou continued to pay the salaries of six of the Affected Employees totalling S$309,376.85 on behalf of PSMPL.
On 12 December 2008, bonuses totalling S$267,127.50 were paid to CC Liu, Chik, Liu Por and Yang.
Between January 2009 and December 2010, Parakou paid a total amount of S$240,000 as rent either to or on behalf of PIH.
On 11 February 2009, Parakou informed Clarkson that it would not be executing the charterparty for the Canton Trader.
The amounts for OPL vessel construction were initially paid for by RP Capital and RP Ventures by way of shareholder’s loans.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether various pre-liquidation transactions involving asset sales, business transfers, and related-party payments resulted in a depletion of assets available to creditors or constituted breaches of directors' duties.
Party_Details.Rule: In company and insolvency law, directors must manage the company in the interests of creditors when the company is insolvent or in serious financial difficulty. This includes avoiding dispositions of assets at an undervalue or creating unfair preferences through related-party transactions.
Party_Details.Application: The pre-dispute conduct shows a pattern of intra-group transactions and structural changes during a period of negative equity and market deterioration. These include the sale of OPL vessels to PIH, substantial repayments to related parties, the termination of ship management agreements, and the continued payment of salaries for staff transferred to another group company.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Liu Cheng Chan

Facts.Text: From the time of incorporation of Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd on 13 October 1995, Liu Cheng Chan was a shareholder and director.
With effect from 1 January 2005, Liu Cheng Chan's shareholding in Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd increased to 80%.
With effect from 31 December 2008, Liu Cheng Chan ceased to be a director of Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd.
From 8 January 1997, Liu Cheng Chan was a director and shareholder of Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 14 November 2008, as a director of Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd, Liu Cheng Chan signed a resolution authorising the sale of ten vessels and two hulls.
On 17 November 2008, as a director of PIH, Liu Cheng Chan signed a resolution resolving that PIH would purchase the vessels from Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd.
On 7 July 2008, Liu Cheng Chan signed a letter of financial support addressed to the auditors stating his intention to provide adequate finance to Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd.
On 20 November 2008, acting for Parakou Shipping SA, Liu Cheng Chan issued a demand letter requesting repayment of all outstanding loans from Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd.
On 12 December 2008, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd paid a bonus of S$100,000 to Liu Cheng Chan.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the actions of Liu Cheng Chan in authorising asset sales, directing related-party repayments, and participating in structural changes expose him to personal liability for breach of duty.
Party_Details.Rule: A person acting as a director or shadow director must ensure company assets are not mismanaged to the detriment of creditors, especially in times of insolvency.
Party_Details.Application: Liu Cheng Chan held controlling positions in Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd and several related entities. He authorised the sale of vessels to a company he controlled, issued a demand for repayment on behalf of another of his companies, and received a substantial bonus while the company faced major liabilities and negative equity.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Chik Sau Kam

Facts.Text: From the time of incorporation of Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd on 13 October 1995, Chik Sau Kam was a shareholder and director.
On 22 December 2008, Chik gave notice of her resignation as director with effect from 31 December 2008.
On 14 November 2008, CC Liu and Chik passed a directors' resolution authorising the sale of ten vessels and two hulls.
On 17 November 2008, CC Liu and Chik, as directors of PIH, signed a resolution resolving to purchase the OPL Vessels from Parakou.
On 12 December 2008, Parakou paid Chik S$80,000 as a bonus.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Chik Sau Kam's participation in asset sales, related-party financing approvals, and receipt of bonuses results in liability for breach of directors' duties.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors are required to consider the interests of creditors when approving asset dispositions and related-party transactions if the company is in financial difficulty.
Party_Details.Application: Chik Sau Kam was a director and shareholder who co-signed resolutions authorising the sale of OPL vessels to a related company (PIH) and subsequently approved that purchase in her capacity as director of PIH. She received a significant bonus while the company's financial state was parlous.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Liu Por

Facts.Text: From 2006, Liu Por was Vice-President of Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd and oversaw its day-to-day operations.
On 22 December 2008, Liu Por was appointed as a director of Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd.
On 17 November 2008, Liu Por signed the documents for the sale of the OPL Vessels to PIH.
Between 12 and 24 November 2008, Liu Por approved the PIH Repayments totalling S$9,812,542.80.
On 23 December 2008, Liu Por signed a resolution noting that 39 employees would be relieved of their duties with effect from 31 December 2008.
On 12 December 2008, Parakou paid Liu Por a bonus of S$39,000.
Between January 2009 and December 2010, Parakou continued to pay the salaries of six staff members technically employed by PSMPL.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the actions of Liu Por in executing intra-group asset transfers, repayments, and salary payments result in personal liability for breach of duty.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in the best interests of the company, and in times of insolvency, this extends to prioritizing the interests of the company's creditors.
Party_Details.Application: Liu Por oversaw day-to-day operations and eventually became a director and majority shareholder. He executed the sale of vessels to a related entity at a value questioned by the liquidator and approved repayments to related parties while the company faced massive contingent liabilities.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Yang Jianguo

Facts.Text: From 2006, Yang Jianguo was President of Parakou and oversaw its day-to-day operations.
On 22 December 2008, Yang Jianguo was appointed as a director and became a 30% shareholder.
On 14 November 2008, as President, Yang Jianguo was authorised to execute documents for the sale of vessels and hulls.
Between 12 and 24 November 2008, Yang Jianguo approved the PIH Repayments totalling S$9,812,542.80.
On 5 December 2008, Yang Jianguo authorised the PSSA Repayment of S$3,046,200.
On 12 December 2008, Parakou paid Yang Jianguo a bonus of S$48,127.50.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Yang Jianguo's role in approving intra-group asset transfers, repayments, and remuneration changes leads to personal liability.
Party_Details.Rule: Individuals who serve as directors are required to act in the interest of the company and its creditors, particularly when the company is in financial distress.
Party_Details.Application: Yang Jianguo served as President and later director. He authorized several large repayments to related parties and received a significant bonus during a period where the company showed negative equity and faced substantial contingent claims.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 8 January 1997.
On 17 November 2008, PIH purchased the OPL Vessels from Parakou for S$9,905,600.
Between 12 and 24 November 2008, Parakou repaid PIH a total amount of S$9,812,542.80 that was owing to PIH.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd's receipt of assets and repayments from Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd constitutes participation in a breach of duty or a transaction at an undervalue.
Party_Details.Rule: A company receiving assets from another company whose assets are managed by fiduciaries may be liable for knowing receipt or dishonest assistance if it benefits from transactions conducted in breach of those fiduciaries' duties.
Party_Details.Application: PIH, controlled by members of the same family as Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd, purchased multiple vessels from the latter and received substantial repayments at a time when the seller was insolvent and facing major claims.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Parakou Shipmanagement Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Parakou Shipmanagement Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 18 November 2008.
In January 2009, multiple employees transferred from Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd were employed by PSMPL.
Between January 2009 and December 2010, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd paid the salaries of six employees technically employed by PSMPL.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Parakou Shipmanagement Pte Ltd's receipt of business and staff, and the payment of its employee salaries by Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd, result in liability.
Party_Details.Rule: A company that benefits from transactions found to be in breach of a fiduciary's duties may be held liable for dishonest assistance or knowing receipt.
Party_Details.Application: PSMPL was incorporated shortly before the termination of management agreements with the original company and then entered into similar agreements with the same entities. It also benefited from having another group company pay the salaries of its own staff.
